I didn’t eat dinner. I consumed raw masculine propaganda. One bite in and my ancestors started nodding in approval. This steak made me feel primal, powerful, and vaguely responsible for defending a village. I feel like taxes should fear me, trees should watch their backs, and I should be chopping wood for no reason. I don’t feel full. I feel chosen. #primalMan #theChosen
I'm listening 👂 #memes

I think I just terrified Jerry 😅

#adventure #mountains #snowboarding #winter

Flew to Calorado on Friday, rented a Tesla, cranked the heat and slept in it for the night, it was -13 degrees Celsius outside of the vehicle.

Woke up already in the mountains -- no drive, no hotel.

Spent the day riding, then parked next to a random hotel lot and slept again in the same car.

Shower? Planet fitness is around the corner.

Next morning I explored Denver a bit, took in the city, and on Sunday returned the car at the airport and flew home.

No days off.
No hotel.

#adventure #mountains #snowboarding

Nothing wrong with having kids-- it’s one of the most meaningful paths in life.

Just don’t do it out of pressure.

It’s not a phase or a trend. It’s a lifetime commitment.

#taylorswift #adventure #snowboarding #mountains #deeptalk

This video isn’t about my snowboarding-- it’s about showcasing my video editing skills.

Thanks for watching this step in the journey toward creating ads that actually make you stop and feel something.

#videoediting #snowboarding #mountsims #adventure

Some people relax on weekends.
I fall on snow repeatedly until it works.

#snowboarding #mountains #adventure

Falling is part of the game.

Getting back up is the skill.

People often change more after travel than after therapy.

Not because therapy doesn’t work -- it does. But environment rewires identity faster than information does.

When you travel, your brain is forced into novelty mode.

New sounds.
New language.
New food.
New rules.
New social cues.

Your brain can’t rely on autopilot anymore.

Novelty activates learning systems in the brain.
Learning reshapes neural pathways.

Suddenly you’re adapting, observing, recalibrating.

And something subtle but powerful happens: In a new country, no one knows who you used to be.

You’re not the anxious one.
Not the quiet one.
Not the version people expect.

You become a blank page in a new environment.

And sometimes that’s exactly what the brain needs to rewrite identity.

You don’t need fancy hotels or a penthouse balcony to travel.

I’ve crossed states and countries, waited seven hours between airports, and slept in my car in sub-zero temperatures. Every moment out there gave me something real -- perspective, stories, lessons you simply can’t get staying comfortable at home.

Be courageous.
Be adventurous.
Don’t be timid.
Don’t be shy.

March 13th, 2026 -- 9:19 AM.
Paraskevidekatriaphobia.

No luck in sight.

A receding hairline.
Hair turning white.

The struggle became desperation.

Disappointed? No.
Angry? Yes.

I went from having no mirrors in my office…
to surrounding myself with them.

I stand there, looking into my own eyes.

Voice: “What the hell are you doing, dude?”
Voice: “You only lose if you stop.”
Voice: “How pathetic would it be to quit now?”

It would be easy to walk away.
To drop everything.

But defeat only happens if I stop.
So I keep going.

Velocity. 10 hours a day.
Velocity. 12 hours a day.
Velocity. 13 hours a day.
Velocity. 14 hours a day. 
.. again.. again.. until the heart stops into the next life.

Thank you, Ahmed.
Thank you, me.

Thank you for believing in me, Ahmed.

Before the day is done…Happy 1st birthday to my brilliant, beautiful baby boy, shaia blue. I am in awe of you. You send pangs of the purest joy through my heart every time I look at you. Thank you for making me a mama. I can already see the type of person you will be. Kind, sweet, observant, curious, playful, hilarious. I am so proud of you. Can you tell I’m in love? Happy birthday my angel!
